duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentVeToken.stake() allows any caller to specify an arbitrary receiver and delegatee, overriding the receiver's ENTIRE existing voting delegation via _delegate(receiver, delegatee) without verifying msg.sender == receiver
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the identical code defect in AgentVeToken.stake() where _delegate(receiver, delegatee) is called with user-supplied receiver without authorization check. ECON-H001 selected for most comprehensive attack economics and impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentFactoryV4.initFromToken allows passing assetToken as tokenAddr; withdraw then transfers ENTIRE factory assetToken balance via IERC20(customToken).balanceOf(address(this)) when customToken == assetToken, draining all depositors
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six threats describe the same two-part vulnerability: (1) missing tokenAddr != assetToken check in initFromToken, and (2) withdraw sending entire balanceOf of custom token. ECON-H002 selected for detailed attack economics including capital requirements and profit margins.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentNftV2.addValidator() is permissionless (no access control) and _initValidatorScore grants new validators a base score equal to totalProposals, enabling free score inflation and governance/reputation corruption
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All ten threats describe the same missing access control on addValidator combined with inflated base score from _initValidatorScore. TECH-H013 selected as most comprehensive, covering both score inflation and unbounded growth aspects. Note: TECH2-H004 refers to the first instance with title 'Permissionless Validator Registration Inflates Scores'; the second instance with title 'AgentToken reentrancy/side-effect risk' is a different vulnerability and is retained.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ValidatorRegistry.totalUptimeScore() has an unbounded loop over all validators; combined with permissionless addValidator, thousands of validators can be registered causing gas DoS
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the same DoS vector: unbounded for-loop in totalUptimeScore iterating over a permissionlessly-growable validator array. ECON-H004 selected for best attack economics. Note: TECH2-H005 refers to the first instance with title 'Unbounded Loop in totalUptimeScore Causes DoS'; the second instance about arbitrary LP pool addresses is a different vulnerability and is retained.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentFactoryV4.withdraw() transfers IERC20(customToken).balanceOf(address(this)) instead of a tracked deposit amount, sending excess tokens (from airdrops, dust, or other sources) to the withdrawer
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same code defect: withdraw uses balanceOf instead of tracking the deposited initialLP amount. Distinct from DUP-002 (assetToken aliasing) as this focuses on the general excess-balance issue without requiring aliasing. TECH-H004 selected for clearest technical description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: setProjectTaxRates has no upper bound validation; tax rates >= BP_DENOM (10000) cause unchecked underflow in _taxProcessing where amountLessTax_ -= tax wraps to near uint256.max, creating phantom token balances
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six threats describe the same arithmetic vulnerability: unbounded tax rates in setProjectTaxRates combined with unchecked subtraction in _taxProcessing causing catastrophic underflow. OPS-H004 also covers monotonicity but its primary critical finding is the underflow. TECH-H006 selected for most precise technical analysis of the unchecked block mechanics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: setProjectTaxRates comment states rates are 'subject to only ever decreasing' but no monotonicity check is enforced, allowing owner/factory to increase tax rates as a rug-pull vector
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same missing monotonicity enforcement in setProjectTaxRates (distinct from the >100% underflow in DUP-006, as this covers rate increases within valid range). ECON-H007 selected for better impact analysis and attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentFactoryV4 uses _tokenApplication[tokenAddr] == 0 as sentinel for 'not registered', but if _nextId is initialized to 0, the first application gets ID 0 which is indistinguishable from the sentinel, enabling duplicate token registration
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six threats describe the same sentinel value collision when _nextId starts at 0. ECON-H017 describes the same root cause with a drain consequence. TECH-H008 selected for most comprehensive analysis. Note: TECH2-H006 refers to the first instance about sentinel collision; the second instance about unlimited ERC20 approvals is a different vulnerability and is retained.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentFactoryV2.executeApplication uses raw IERC20(assetToken).transfer() instead of SafeERC20.safeTransfer() for initial liquidity funding, risking silent failure with non-standard ERC20 tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six threats describe the identical unsafe transfer pattern in AgentFactory.sol executeApplication. TECH-H009 selected for clearest technical analysis noting V3/V4 correctly use safeTransfer.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentToken._mint uses _totalSupply += uint128(amount) while _balances uses uint256; the maxSupply validation checks raw units not decimal-scaled amounts, so scaled amounts can exceed uint128.max causing silent truncation and supply/balance divergence
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same uint128 truncation issue in _mint/_burn with inadequate pre-decimal validation in _processSupplyParams. ECON2-H011 extends to _burn but same root cause. TECH-H010 selected for most detailed analysis of the scaling gap.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentFactoryV4._createPair requires pair doesn't exist; an attacker can front-run executeTokenApplication by creating the Uniswap pair directly, permanently blocking execution
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same front-running DoS on custom token application execution via permissionless Uniswap pair creation. TECH-H011 selected for more detailed analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentToken._swapTax passes amountOutMin=0 to Uniswap swap, providing zero slippage protection and enabling sandwich/MEV extraction of all tax revenue
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four describe the same zero-slippage vulnerability in _swapTax. ECON-H005 selected for comprehensive economics. Note: TECH2-H003 refers to the first instance titled 'Atomic Sandwich Attack on Tax Autoswap'; the second instance titled 'Uniswap router failure...stuck _autoSwapInProgress' is a different vulnerability and is retained.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentNftV2.setDAO allows the current DAO to set a new DAO address to any value (including address(0) or a malicious contract) without validation, permanently bricking or hijacking governance
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same missing validation in setDAO. TECH-H014 focuses on address(0) bricking, TECH2-H009 covers both address(0) and malicious contract hijacking. TECH2-H009 selected as more comprehensive. Note: this refers to the first TECH2-H009 instance titled 'Permanent DAO Hijacking via setDAO'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentFactoryV3.initFromBondingCurve accepts a caller-supplied creator parameter set as application.proposer without validation, enabling either the creator to steal BONDING_ROLE deposits or BONDING_ROLE to impersonate arbitrary creators
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both target the same design flaw in initFromBondingCurve where the payer (msg.sender) and beneficiary (creator/proposer) are separated without validation. ECON-H016 selected for more concrete exploit scenario with clearer economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentToken.botProtectionDurationInSeconds is stored during initialize but never referenced in any transfer logic, rendering bot protection completely non-functional (dead code)
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five describe the identical dead code issue: botProtectionDurationInSeconds is set but never checked in transfer paths. TECH-H005 selected for clear and focused technical analysis with precise code references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentMigrator.migrateAgent uses raw IERC20(_assetToken).transferFrom() instead of SafeERC20.safeTransferFrom(), risking silent failure with non-standard ERC20 tokens and broken migrations
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same unsafe transferFrom pattern in AgentMigrator.migrateAgent. OPS-H018 selected for more detailed operational impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentToken.distributeTaxTokens is permissionless, allowing anyone to force direct transfer of accumulated tax tokens to projectTaxRecipient, bypassing the intended autoswap-to-pairToken workflow
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same permissionless distributeTaxTokens function enabling bypass of the intended autoswap mechanism. OPS2-H005 selected for clearer description.
